steps.title: Follow opinion to behavior
steps.content: The text first reports favorable opinions of Mr. Ely: laypeople admire his powers and learning, and other clergy find him discreet and agreeable. It then lists his careful habits, such as avoiding heated discussion and ridicule.

steps.title: Describe the structure
steps.content: The passage moves from others' favorable view of Mr. Ely to the behaviors that help him preserve that view. A invents a decline, B invents naivete, and C claims the text explains his motivation. The answer is D.
